explanation: The LTL property ensures that the robot will always explain why it placed an object in a specific location. It uses the 'G' (Globally) operator to ensure that this explanation happens continuously over time. The atomic proposition 'explainObjectLocation' directly represents the action of the robot explaining the object's location.

The LTL formula ensures that if the robot places an object, it will eventually explain why it placed the object in that specific location. The formula uses the 'objectPlaced' and 'explainObjectLocation' atomic propositions.
This LTL property states that it is always the case that if the robot has successfully placed an object, it must explain the location of the object.

The LTL property ensures that whenever the robot has placed an object ('objectPlaced') and needs to explain its object's location ('explainObjectLocation'), it will persistently provide the explanation ('explain'). The boolean expressions ensure the property correctly interprets the atomic propositions.
This LTL property states that the robot should always explain why it put an object in a specific location. It uses the atomic proposition 'explainObjectLocation' to indicate the need for explanation.
This LTL property states that the robot should always explain why it put an object in a specific location.

This LTL property states that the robot should always provide an explanation for why it placed an object in a specific location.  The property ensures that in every state, if the robot has placed an object, it must provide an explanation.

This LTL formula states that it is always the case that if the robot places an object (objectPlaced), it provides an explanation (explainObjectLocation) for why it put the object in that specific location. N_DURATION is set to 1 to represent the immediate next state.

This LTL property states that globally, if the robot has placed an object (objectPlaced), it must explain the object's location (explainObjectLocation). Since this is a basic requirement with no condition, the template 7 is chosen.

This LTL property states that if the robot has successfully placed an object (objectPlaced) and has been requested to explain its location (explainObjectLocation), then eventually within a specified duration (N_DURATION), the robot should provide a detailed explanation of the location (detailRequested and explain).

The LTL property ensures that globally, if the robot has placed an object ('objectPlaced') and is explaining the object's location ('explainObjectLocation'), then it will always be in a state where the explanation is detailed ('detailRequested').

This LTL property states that it is always the case that if the robot explains why it put an object in a specific location (explainObjectLocation), then it is always the case that the explanation is detailed (detailed) or an alternative is provided which is also detailed (explainAlternatives & detailed). The property uses a simple implication to model the requirement and is always true. The value of N_DURATION is not relevant in this case, as the template does not include any time bound operators.

This LTL property states that globally, if the robot has placed an object and the user requests a detailed explanation, then the robot should provide an explanation of why it placed the object in that location, either immediately or within a certain time period (N_DURATION).
This LTL property states that it is always the case that if the robot has successfully placed an object, then it must always explain why it put the object in that location.

This LTL property states that globally, if the robot places an object (objectPlaced), then the robot should also explain why it put the object in a specific location (explainObjectLocation). N_DURATION is set to 1.
This LTL property states that it is always the case that if the robot has placed an object, then it must always explain the location where the object was placed.

This LTL property states that the robot should always explain why it put an object in a specific location. The property uses the atomic proposition 'explainObjectLocation' to represent this requirement. The LTL formula ensures that the robot continuously provides this explanation.

This LTL property states that it is always the case that if the robot has placed an object (objectPlaced), then it will always explain the location of the object (explainObjectLocation) for a period of N_DURATION steps. This captures the requirement that the robot provides an explanation for placing an object in a specific location.

This LTL formula states that it is always the case that if the robot places an object (objectPlaced), then it should always explain (explainObjectLocation) why that object was placed in a specific location, with an explanation that should be always provided for the next N_DURATION time steps. This captures the request to explain the object's location.

This LTL property states that the robot should always provide an explanation for why it put an object in a specific location. The explanation will be given indefinitely from the point when the robot places the object.
This LTL property states that globally, if the robot places an object, then it will eventually explain why it put the object in that specific location. N_DURATION is set to 5.

This LTL property states that it should always be the case that if the robot has successfully placed an object, it should explain why it put an object in a specific location. The LTL template G(bool_exp1 -> bool_exp3) is used to encode this relationship.

This LTL property states that it is always the case that if the robot has successfully placed an object and if the robot is explaining the object's location, then it will always explain the object location within a certain time window (N_DURATION).

This LTL property states that it is always the case that if the robot places an object in a specific location ('objectPlaced'), then it should explain why it put the object there ('explainObjectLocation') for a period of time.  The 'G' operator ensures this happens at all times, and the duration is indicated by 'N_DURATION'.

This LTL property states that it should always be the case (G) that if the robot places an object (objectPlaced), then it will always be the case (G) that if detailRequested is true, the robot will explain (explainObjectLocation) why the object was placed in the specific location.

This LTL property states that globally, if the robot is in manipulation mode (manipulationMode) and has placed an object (objectPlaced), then it must provide an explanation (explainObjectLocation) of the object's location. The property uses the 'G' (Globally) operator to ensure this holds true throughout the entire execution. N_DURATION has no effect in this case.

This LTL property states that globally (G), if the robot has placed an object (objectPlaced), and the system is in a decision-making state (decisionMaking), then it will explain why it put the object in that specific location (explainObjectLocation) or the system is always explainObjectLocation. N_DURATION does not matter in this case.

The LTL formula states that it is always the case that if the robot has successfully placed an object (objectPlaced), then it will always explain why it put the object in that location (explainObjectLocation) for a given duration. The 'G' (Globally) operator ensures this holds true at all times.

This LTL property states that globally, if the robot places an object in a specific location (explainObjectLocation), then it must provide an explanation for that action. The property utilizes an 'always' operator (G) to enforce this requirement throughout the robot's operation. The chosen template uses 'bool_exp3' to represent explainObjectLocation, which becomes the main focus of the property.
This LTL property states that globally, if the robot has successfully placed an object, then it will always explain why it put an object in a specific location.

This LTL property states that globally (G), if the robot is in manipulation mode, then it provides an explanation (explainObjectLocation) regarding the object's placement. The LTL template G(bool_exp2 -> (G bool_exp3)) is used to capture this.

This LTL property states that globally, whenever the robot places an object (objectPlaced), it will eventually explain why it put the object in that specific location (explainObjectLocation). The explanation provided does not require any specific conditions.

This LTL property ensures that globally, whenever the robot has successfully placed an object, it will eventually explain why it placed the object in that specific location. The property utilizes the temporal operator F (eventually) to describe this behavior. The use of G (globally) makes sure this applies to all time steps.

This LTL property states that it is always the case (G) that if the robot has successfully placed an object (objectPlaced), then the robot explains why it put an object in a specific location (explainObjectLocation). This ensures that the robot provides an explanation for its actions when placing objects.

This LTL formula states that globally, if the robot has placed an object, then the robot should always explain why it put the object in the specific location. The choice of template captures the requirement that the explanation is provided whenever the object placement occurs.

This LTL property states that globally, if the robot has placed an object (objectPlaced), then the robot must explain (explainObjectLocation). The property also states that the explanation should be detailed (detailed).

This LTL property states that globally, if the robot has placed an object and the user requests an explanation about the location, the robot must explain the object's location, or the explanation should continue to be provided for some duration. The formula ensures that the explanation is provided.

The LTL property ensures that if the robot is explaining, and has placed an object, it must provide an explanation for why the object was placed in that location. This is captured by the use of the G (globally) operator over the implication, which asserts that the explanation of the object location always occurs, given the other conditions are met.

This LTL property states that globally, if the robot has placed an object (objectPlaced), then the robot must always explain why it put the object in that location (explainObjectLocation) over a period of N_DURATION time steps. It uses a conditional statement to ensure that the explanation is provided when the action occurs.

This LTL formula states that globally, if the robot is placing an object, the robot must explain the location where the object is put, or will explain the location where the object is put in the next step, within a certain time window (N_DURATION).

This LTL formula states that globally, if the robot is in a state where it is placing an object, then it should provide an explanation for the object's location. Specifically, if object placement happens at any time step, the explanation should start at that time or in the future for a certain duration.The use of the future operator ensures that the explanation starts immediately and will be provided within the specified time frame(N_DURATION).
This LTL property states that globally, if the robot has placed an object and provides a reason, then the robot will explain the object's location within a certain time frame (N_DURATION).

This LTL property states that globally, if the robot has successfully placed an object and detailRequested, then it must always provide an explanation for placing the object in that specific location.

This LTL property states that globally, if the robot has placed an object, then the robot should explain why it put an object in a specific location.  The property uses the "G" (Globally) operator to ensure that this condition holds true throughout the robot's operation. The boolean expressions are defined in terms of the atomic propositions provided.

This LTL property states that the robot must always explain why it put an object in a specific location.
This LTL property states that globally, if the robot places an object, it should always explain why it put the object in that location.  The explanation must be detailed in nature.

The LTL property states that, whenever the robot has placed an object ('objectPlaced'), it must explain why it placed the object in the current location ('explainObjectLocation'). The property uses a temporal operator 'G' (Globally), which means this requirement holds true at all times. The duration is not specified in this case, but we will set a reasonable duration for explanation to be 3.

This LTL property states that globally (G), if the robot has placed an object (objectPlaced), then the robot must explain (explainObjectLocation) why it put the object in that location, and in the future (X).

This LTL property states that it is always the case that if the robot places an object in a specific location (explainObjectLocation), then it will always provide a detailed explanation about the current location (explainLocation) or the robot provides an explanation. N_DURATION is set to 5, indicating that this should happen within a short time frame.
